full_text:
No rain in sight as Hyderabad gears up for heatwave
Heads up, Hyderabad, get ready for a couple of toasty, dry days ahead.
Temperatures are set to hit 32 to 34 degrees Celsius with no rain in sight, associated with a trough stretching from Bihar across Jharkhand, Chhattisgarh and Vidarbha to Marathwada.
Nights will stay warm too, hovering around 21 degrees Celsius.
Authorities recommend drinking plenty of water or coconut water
With the UV Index spiking at nine (that's pretty high) and humidity sitting at 40% to 45%, it's easy to get dehydrated or sunburned, especially if you're outdoors.
Authorities recommend drinking plenty of water or coconut water, wearing light cotton clothes, and using sunglasses or umbrellas if you have to step out.
If you're planning outdoor activities or farm work, try to wrap them up early in the morning and avoid being outside between 11am and 4pm
Stay cool and take care!

full_text:
After Heat Spell, Thunderstorms Likely in Telangana Between March 16–23
A shift in weather patterns is expected to bring temporary relief from the intense heat prevailing across Telangana , even as meteorologists warn of thunderstorms and rain later this month. After several days of rising temperatures across the state, experts say atmospheric conditions are beginning to change, which could slightly ease the heat in the coming days.
According to weather forecasts, maximum temperatures across Telangana are likely to remain in the range of 35°C to 37°C in several districts over the next few days. In Hyderabad , daytime temperatures are expected to hover around 34°C to 35°C , while eastern parts of the state may record slightly higher readings near 36°C to 37°C . The brief moderation in temperatures between March 9 and March 11 may offer some relief to residents who have been experiencing unusually warm conditions for early March.
However, meteorologists indicate that the weather could turn more active in the following week. Forecasts suggest that between March 16 and March 23 , several regions in Telangana may experience thunderstorms accompanied by strong winds, lightning, and scattered rainfall . In some areas, hailstorms are also possible, particularly during intense storm activity triggered by atmospheric instability.
Such weather patterns are commonly seen during the seasonal transition from winter to summer , when rising temperatures combine with moisture and wind shifts to create conditions favourable for convective storms . These storms can produce short spells of heavy rain, gusty winds and lightning, occasionally affecting agriculture and outdoor activities.
Officials have advised farmers across the state to remain vigilant and take preventive measures to safeguard standing crops from potential damage caused by strong winds, lightning, or hail. Farmers have been urged to monitor weather advisories closely and make necessary arrangements to minimise risks during the expected spell of unstable weather.
While the forecast rain could provide some relief from the early summer heat, authorities caution that the accompanying thunderstorms may lead to localised disruptions in parts of Telangana over the coming days.
